rulings.Case Title: TRINITY MANAGEMENT SERVICES, INC VS. MANUEL NAVARRO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 30, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANTS MANUEL NAVARRO AND CHENOA THORNTON'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is transferred to department 210 to be heard on August 31, 2021 at 11:00 a.m. per the order of the presiding judge. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BPREP MOSSO APARTMENTS LLC VS. KAYLA HOLLINGSWORTH ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 30, 2021 line 3. DEFENDANT KAYLA HOLLINGSWORTH'S FINANCIAL DISTRESS DECLARATION Hearing Required to address whether tenant's failure to timely return financial distress declaration was the result of mistake, inadvertence, surprise or excusable neglect. CCP 1179.04(h)(1)(B) =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
